name: Click
description: Python package for creating command-line interfaces with composable commands, automatic help, and type validation.

name: clap
description: Rust command-line argument parser. Derive macros, subcommands, shell completions, and auto-generated help.

name: Cobra
description: Go library for creating CLI applications. Powers kubectl, Hugo, and GitHub CLI. Subcommands and auto-completions.

name: argparse
description: Python's built-in command-line argument parser. Subcommands, type checking, and automatic help generation.

name: yargs
description: Node.js CLI argument parser. Generates help text, parses commands, and validates inputs with a fluent API.

name: oclif
description: Framework for building CLIs in Node.js by Salesforce. Plugins, hooks, auto-documentation, and TypeScript support.

name: Commander.js
description: Complete solution for Node.js command-line interfaces. Options, subcommands, and auto-generated help pages.

name: Typer
description: Build CLI apps in Python using type hints. Built on Click with auto-completion, help, and colorized output.

name: picocli
description: Java CLI framework using annotations. POSIX/GNU syntax, colorized help, subcommands, and GraalVM native image support.

name: Python Fire
description: Google library that generates CLIs from any Python object. Turn functions, classes, and modules into commands automatically.

name: urfave/cli
description: Declarative Go package for building CLIs. Commands, flags, shell completions for bash/zsh/fish, and env var lookups.

name: docopt
description: CLI argument parser that generates parsers from help message text. Write the usage pattern, get the parser for free.

name: Clikt
description: Multiplatform Kotlin CLI library. Type-safe parameters, nested commands, shell autocomplete, and JVM/native/JS targets.

name: System.CommandLine
description: Microsoft's .NET library for building CLIs. POSIX/Windows conventions, tab completion, response files, and AOT support.

name: Bubble Tea
description: TUI framework for Go based on The Elm Architecture. Build interactive terminal apps with components and state management.

name: Ink
description: Build interactive CLI apps with React components. Uses Yoga layout engine for flexbox-based terminal UIs.

name: Rich
description: Python library for rich text and formatting in the terminal. Tables, progress bars, syntax highlighting, and Markdown.

name: Textual
description: TUI framework for Python by the Rich author. Build sophisticated terminal apps with CSS-like styling and widgets.

name: Tokio
description: Async runtime for Rust. Event-driven, non-blocking I/O with task scheduling, timers, and networking primitives.

name: Rayon
description: Data parallelism library for Rust. Convert sequential iterators to parallel with a single method call.

name: asyncio
description: Python's built-in async I/O framework. Event loop, coroutines, tasks, and streams for concurrent programming.

name: Trio
description: Friendly Python library for async concurrency. Structured concurrency with cancellation, timeouts, and nurseries.

name: errgroup
description: Go package for synchronizing goroutines. Propagates errors and cancels groups on first failure.

name: RxJS
description: Reactive extensions for JavaScript. Compose async and event-based programs using observable sequences and operators.

name: AnyIO
description: Structured concurrency library that works on top of asyncio and Trio. Write async code compatible with both runtimes.

name: smol
description: Small and fast async runtime for Rust. Lightweight alternative to Tokio with a simple executor and reactor.

name: Crossbeam
description: Concurrency tools for Rust. Lock-free data structures, scoped threads, channels, and epoch-based memory reclamation.

name: concurrent.futures
description: Python stdlib module for launching parallel tasks. ThreadPoolExecutor and ProcessPoolExecutor with a simple Future-based API.

name: tracing
description: Structured diagnostics and instrumentation for Rust. Spans, events, and subscribers for async-aware logging.

name: zap
description: Blazing fast structured logging for Go by Uber. Zero-allocation in hot paths with both sugared and typed APIs.

name: Winston
description: Multi-transport async logging library for Node.js. Custom log levels, formatters, and output to files, console, or HTTP.

name: Pino
description: Very low overhead Node.js logger. JSON output, child loggers, and transports. Fastest logger for Node.js.

name: slog
description: Go's standard library structured logging package. Leveled logging with JSON and text handlers built in.

name: Loguru
description: Python logging made simple. No boilerplate, colorized output, exception catching, and file rotation out of the box.

name: zerolog
description: Zero-allocation JSON logger for Go. Chained API for building log events with minimal memory overhead.

name: structlog
description: Structured logging for Python. Processors pipeline, key-value pairs, and integration with stdlib logging and Loguru.

name: env_logger
description: Rust logger configured via environment variables. Filter log output by module path and level using RUST_LOG.

name: Bunyan
description: JSON logging library for Node.js. Structured log records, log levels, child loggers, and a CLI for pretty-printing.

name: log4rs
description: Highly configurable logging framework for Rust inspired by log4j. File appenders, rolling policies, and YAML config.

name: Log4j 2
description: Java logging framework by Apache. Async loggers, pluggable appenders, filters, and pattern layouts for enterprise apps.

name: anyhow
description: Flexible error handling for Rust applications. Easy error context, downcasting, and backtraces without boilerplate.

name: thiserror
description: Derive macro for the standard Error trait in Rust. Define custom error types with display messages and source chains.

name: eyre
description: Flexible error reporting library for Rust. Customizable error reports with context, backtraces, and span traces.

name: neverthrow
description: Type-safe error handling for TypeScript. Result and ResultAsync types inspired by Rust, eliminating try-catch.

name: ts-results
description: Rust-like Result and Option types for TypeScript. Pattern matching, map, and unwrap for safer error handling.

name: error-stack
description: Context-aware error handling library for Rust by Hash. Attach context, build error chains, and generate rich reports.

name: miette
description: Diagnostic reporting library for Rust. Renders beautiful error messages with source code snippets, labels, and help text.

name: SNAFU
description: Rust library for assigning context to errors. Generates error types with derive macros and structured context selectors.

name: Effect
description: TypeScript library for typed functional programming. Treats errors as values with tracked error channels, retry, and recovery.

name: returns
description: Typed functional containers for Python. Result, Maybe, IO, and Future monads for explicit error handling without exceptions.

name: result
description: Simple Result type for Python inspired by Rust. Return Ok or Err values instead of raising exceptions, fully type-annotated.

name: kotlin-result
description: Multiplatform Result monad for Kotlin. Models success or failure with type-safe error handling, replacing try-catch patterns.

name: Vavr
description: Functional library for Java with Try, Either, and Option types. Immutable collections and pattern matching for safer code.

name: Arrow (Kotlin)
description: Typed functional programming library for Kotlin. Either, Option, and Raise DSL for composable, type-safe error handling.

name: language-ext
description: Functional programming framework for C#. Option, Either, Try, and Fin monads for declarative error handling without nulls.

name: color-eyre
description: Colorful error reporting for Rust. Builds on eyre with pretty-printed backtraces, span traces, and custom sections.

name: oxide.ts
description: Rust-inspired Result and Option types for TypeScript. Pattern matching, unwrap, map, and combinators for safe error handling.

name: Boost.Outcome
description: C++ library for lightweight error handling using Result/Outcome types. Deterministic, no-exception alternative to throwing.

name: Elm Error Handling
description: Elm's built-in Result and Maybe types for error handling without exceptions. Compiler-enforced exhaustive pattern matching.

name: date-fns
description: Modern JavaScript date utility library. Modular, tree-shakeable functions for parsing, formatting, and comparing dates.

name: Luxon
description: Powerful JavaScript date/time library by a Moment.js maintainer. Immutable, built on Intl API, with time zones and durations.

name: Day.js
description: Lightweight JavaScript date library with the same API as Moment.js. Only 2KB, immutable, and plugin-based.

name: Moment.js
description: Legacy JavaScript date library for parsing, validating, and formatting dates. Widely used but now in maintenance mode.

name: chrono
description: Date and time library for Rust. Timezone-aware types, parsing, formatting, and arithmetic with nanosecond precision.

name: Arrow
description: Better dates and times for Python. Human-friendly API for creating, manipulating, and formatting datetime objects.

name: Pendulum
description: Python datetimes made easy. Drop-in replacement for datetime with timezone handling, periods, and human-readable diffs.

name: Noda Time
description: Alternative date and time API for .NET. Clearer separation of concepts like instants, local dates, and durations.

name: Joda-Time
description: Quality date and time library for Java prior to java.time. Immutable types, timezone handling, and interval support.

name: fzf
description: General-purpose command-line fuzzy finder. Integrates with shell history, file search, and git branches.

name: ripgrep
description: Extremely fast recursive text search tool. Respects gitignore, supports regex, and replaces grep for most uses.

name: fd
description: Simple, fast alternative to find. Intuitive syntax, regex support, and respects gitignore by default.

name: bat
description: A cat clone with syntax highlighting, line numbers, and Git integration. Drop-in replacement for cat.

name: The Silver Searcher
description: Code-searching tool similar to ack, but faster. Ignores VCS files, respects .gitignore, and searches recursively.

name: eza
description: Modern replacement for ls. Colors, icons, git status, tree view, and extended file metadata in a fast Rust binary.

name: broot
description: Interactive tree view for navigating directories. Fuzzy search, file preview, and custom verbs for file operations.

name: zoxide
description: Smarter cd command that learns your habits. Tracks frequently used directories and jumps to them with partial matches.

name: tmux
description: Terminal multiplexer. Multiple sessions, windows, and panes in one terminal. Persistent sessions survive disconnects.

name: Zsh
description: Extended Bourne shell with many improvements. Themes, plugins, spelling correction, and programmable completion.

name: Oh My Zsh
description: Community-driven framework for managing Zsh configuration. 300+ plugins, 150+ themes, and auto-update.

name: Fish
description: User-friendly command-line shell. Syntax highlighting, autosuggestions, and tab completions that work out of the box.

name: Starship
description: Minimal, fast, and customizable shell prompt. Works with any shell, shows git status, language versions, and more.

name: Pandoc
description: Universal document converter. Markdown, LaTeX, HTML, DOCX, EPUB, and many more. The Swiss army knife of docs.

name: Calibre
description: E-book management application with format conversion between EPUB, MOBI, PDF, and 20+ formats. Built-in editor and viewer.

name: WeasyPrint
description: Python library to convert HTML and CSS to PDF. Handles pagination, headers, footers, and print-quality output.

name: wkhtmltopdf
description: Command-line tool to render HTML to PDF using the WebKit engine. Supports headers, footers, and JavaScript rendering.

name: Markdownify
description: Python library to convert HTML to Markdown. Handles tables, images, links, and nested elements cleanly.

name: LibreOffice CLI
description: LibreOffice headless mode for batch document conversion. Convert between DOCX, PDF, ODT, and many formats via CLI.

name: Asciidoctor
description: Fast text processor for converting AsciiDoc to HTML5, PDF, EPUB, and other formats. Extensible with Ruby or JVM.

name: Typst
description: Modern typesetting system and markup language. Faster than LaTeX with a friendlier syntax for academic and technical docs.

name: Docutils
description: Python documentation utilities for converting reStructuredText to HTML, LaTeX, man pages, and other formats.

name: Lodash
description: Utility library providing helpers for arrays, objects, strings, and functions. The most depended-on npm package.

name: uuid
description: Generate RFC-compliant UUIDs in JavaScript. Supports v1 (timestamp), v4 (random), v5 (namespace), and more.

name: Faker.js
description: Generate realistic fake data for testing. Names, addresses, emails, images, and 50+ data categories.

name: Ramda
description: Functional utility library for JavaScript. Immutable, auto-curried, point-free functions for data transformation.

name: Underscore.js
description: Functional programming helpers for JavaScript. 100+ utility functions for collections, arrays, objects, and functions.

name: dotenv
description: Load environment variables from .env files into process.env. Zero-dependency module for twelve-factor apps.

name: nanoid
description: Tiny, secure, URL-friendly unique string ID generator for JavaScript. Smaller and faster than UUID.

name: itertools
description: Extra iterator adaptors and methods for Rust. Combinations, permutations, grouping, joining, and more beyond std.

name: more-itertools
description: Additional building blocks for working with Python iterables. 100+ recipes beyond the stdlib itertools module.

name: Guava
description: Google's core Java libraries. Collections, caching, primitives, concurrency, I/O, and string processing utilities.

name: Toolz
description: Functional utilities for Python iterators, functions, and dictionaries. Compose, pipe, memoize, and curry without dependencies.

name: Apache Commons
description: Reusable Java components for strings, collections, I/O, math, and more. The standard utility library for Java projects.

name: Kotlin Standard Library
description: Kotlin's core extensions for collections, sequences, text, ranges, and I/O. Concise APIs that complement the JDK.

name: Arduino
description: Open-source electronics platform with IDE, boards, and libraries. Program microcontrollers in C/C++ for hardware projects.

name: Raspberry Pi OS
description: Official operating system for Raspberry Pi. Debian-based Linux optimized for the Pi's ARM hardware.

name: PlatformIO
description: Cross-platform IDE and build system for embedded development. Supports 1000+ boards, debugging, and unit testing.

name: MicroPython
description: Lean implementation of Python 3 for microcontrollers. Run Python on ESP32, STM32, Raspberry Pi Pico, and more.

name: Zephyr
description: Scalable real-time operating system for resource-constrained devices. Supports 500+ boards with safety certifications.

name: ESP-IDF
description: Official development framework for ESP32 chips by Espressif. FreeRTOS-based with Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and peripheral drivers.

name: Tasmota
description: Open-source firmware for ESP8266/ESP32 devices. Control smart home devices via MQTT, web UI, or serial commands.

name: Home Assistant
description: Open-source home automation platform. Integrates 2000+ smart home devices with automations, dashboards, and voice control.

name: Ratatui
description: Rust library for building terminal user interfaces. Successor to tui-rs with widgets, layouts, and an active community.

name: tview
description: Rich interactive terminal UI library for Go. Tables, forms, trees, flexbox layout, and mouse support.

name: ncurses
description: Classic C library for building text-based UIs on Unix terminals. Foundation for countless TUI applications.

name: blessed-contrib
description: Terminal dashboard library for Node.js built on blessed. Charts, maps, gauges, and sparklines in the terminal.

name: FTXUI
description: C++ functional terminal UI library. Components, DOM-like elements, animations, and flexbox layout for modern TUIs.

name: crossterm
description: Cross-platform terminal manipulation library for Rust. Cursor, style, events, and raw mode across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

name: Prompts
description: Lightweight, beautiful interactive prompts for Node.js CLIs. Text input, select, multiselect, confirm, and more.

name: regex101
description: Web-based regex tester and debugger. Explains each token, highlights matches in real-time, supports PCRE, JavaScript, Python, Go, and Java flavors.

name: chardet
description: Python character encoding detector. CLI tool (chardetect) and library for auto-detecting text file encodings from byte sequences.

name: uchardet
description: C/C++ encoding detector based on Mozilla's universal charset detection. Fast CLI tool for identifying text file encodings.

name: uniname
description: Unix CLI tool for looking up Unicode character names, codepoints, and properties. Part of the uniutils package.

name: GNU sed
description: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text. The standard Unix tool for search-and-replace, line deletion, and text manipulation in pipelines.

name: GNU Awk
description: Pattern-scanning and text-processing language. Splits input into records and fields, applies rules, and produces reports. A Unix essential.

name: yq
description: Lightweight command-line YAML, JSON, XML, CSV, and TOML processor. Uses jq-like syntax for querying and transforming structured data files.

name: sd
description: Intuitive find-and-replace CLI. Modern sed alternative with regex and literal modes, cleaner syntax, and significantly faster performance.

name: choose
description: Human-friendly alternative to cut and awk for field selection. Negative indexing, regex separators, and Python-like slice syntax.

name: sttr
description: CLI tool for string transformations. Encode/decode, hash, case conversion, JSON/YAML conversion, and 30+ operations with interactive or piped usage.

name: World Time Buddy
description: Web-based timezone converter with visual overlap grid. Compare multiple timezones side-by-side for scheduling across cities.

name: pycountry
description: Python library providing ISO databases for countries (3166), languages (639), currencies (4217), and subdivisions.

name: Consul
description: Service networking platform by HashiCorp. Service discovery, health checking, KV store, and multi-datacenter configuration.

name: etcd
description: Strongly consistent distributed key-value store for shared configuration and service discovery. Powers Kubernetes.

name: Apache ZooKeeper
description: Centralized service for distributed configuration, naming, synchronization, and group services. Foundation for Kafka and HBase.

name: Flagsmith
description: Open-source feature flag and remote configuration platform. Manage feature rollouts, A/B tests, and config values without deploys.

name: Viper
description: Complete configuration solution for Go apps. Reads from files, env vars, flags, and remote stores. Supports JSON, TOML, YAML, and live reloading.

name: Dynaconf
description: Configuration management for Python with layered settings. Supports TOML, YAML, JSON, env vars, and Django/Flask integration.

name: confd
description: Lightweight configuration management tool. Keeps local config files in sync with data from etcd, Consul, Vault, and other backends.
